title: datura rubra
intro: Datura rubra, commonly known as Red Datura, is a species of flowering plant in the nightshade family that is native to the southwestern United States, Mexico, and parts of Central America. This plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, providing health benefits such as relief from pain, inflammation, and fever, as well as treating respiratory issues like bronchitis and asthma. The therapeutic actions of Datura rubra include its antispasmodic, anti-inflammatory, and expectorant properties, which help to ease muscle spasms, reduce swelling, and promote the clearance of mucus from the lungs. The bioactive constituents of Datura rubra include alkaloids, glycosides, and other compounds like scopolamine, hyoscyamine, and daturine, which contribute to its medicinal effects.
parts: Datura rubra has many medicinal parts, such as the roots, leaves, flowers, and seeds. The roots of Datura rubra are believed to contain medicinal constituents like alkaloids, including scopolamine and hyoscyamine, which are used to treat various ailments. The leaves of the plant are said to contain compounds like solanine and scopolin, which have been traditionally used to treat conditions like fever and rheumatism. The flowers and seeds of Datura rubra are also rich in alkaloids, including atropine and hyoscyamine, which have been used to treat conditions like glaucoma and epilepsy.
side_effects: Datura rubra can have side effects if used improperly, such as hallucinations, delirium, and disorientation. Prolonged use or excessive consumption of the plant can lead to anxiety, panic attacks, and even psychosis in severe cases. Additionally, Datura rubra can cause dry mouth, blurred vision, and increased heart rate due to its anticholinergic properties, which can be particularly problematic for individuals with pre-existing heart conditions. In extreme cases, improper use of Datura rubra can lead to respiratory failure, seizures, and even death.
